methods.description: Process for collecting and preparing radiolarian shells from core samples and living specimens for microscopic observation and measurement.

steps.description: Shells of Acrosphaera cyrtodon were obtained from core samples collected from the central Indian Ocean Basin at a water depth of 5,300 m.
steps.searchable_summary: collect core samples from central Indian Ocean Basin

steps.description: The shells were recovered from 20 cm core depth and prepared for light microscopic observation as a permanent mounted slide.
steps.searchable_summary: prepare core samples for microscopic observation

steps.description: Photomicrographs were made using a binocular microscope and photographed at 200x magnification.
All measurements of shell diameters and pore sizes were made with a Leica microscope and graduated reticule at 250x magnification.
steps.searchable_summary: measure shell diameters and pore sizes at 250x magnification

steps.description: Living colonial radiolaria were collected by SCUBA divers using hand-held polycarbonate jars or by plankton sampling using gentle net tows.
steps.searchable_summary: collect living radiolaria using SCUBA and net tows

steps.description: Living colonial radiolaria were observed and photographed with a dissecting microscope using dark field illumination.
steps.searchable_summary: photograph living radiolaria with dark field illumination
